types.name: BankAccount
types.description: (FROZEN) Defines a single bank account for the corresponding Firm.

fields.name: id
fields.description: A unique uuidv4 value for this object.

fields.name: category
fields.description: The category of the bank account. Either 'operating' or 'trust'.

fields.name: isChargebackAccount
fields.description: Indicates if the BankAccount is used for chargebacks for the firm.

fields.name: isDefault
fields.description: Indicates if the BankAccount is the default for it's category.

fields.name: isFeeAccount
fields.description: Indicates if the BankAccount is used for billing the firm.

fields.name: lastFour
fields.description: The last four digits of the bank account. Example: '1234'.

fields.name: nickname
fields.description: The nickname of this bank account normally set by the Firm.

types.name: ID
types.description: The `ID` scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable. When expected as an input type, any string (such as `"4"`) or integer (such as `4`) input value will be accepted as an ID.

types.name: String
types.description: The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

types.name: Boolean
types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.

fields.name: verifyStatus
fields.deprecationReason: This field is no longer used

fields.name: brandingPageUrl
fields.description: A url to the Confido Legal UI branding page for customizing the payment page.

types.name: Address
types.description:
(FROZEN) An Address is in various entities (Client, Contact, etc.) to define where
the entity is located.

fields.name: id
fields.description: A unique uuidv4 value for this object.

fields.name: city
fields.description: (Nullable) The city for this Address.

fields.name: line1
fields.description: (Nullable) The first line for this Address.

fields.name: line2
fields.description: (Nullable) The second line for this Address.

fields.name: state
fields.description: (Nullable) The state for this Address.

fields.name: zip
fields.description: (Nullable) The zip code for this Address.

types.name: Float
types.description: The `Float` scalar type represents signed double-precision fractional values as specified by [IEEE 754](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IEEE_floating_point).

types.name: StoredPaymentMethod
types.description:
A Stored Payment Method is a payment method (credit card, ACH, etc.) that has been saved to be charged
later in the future. A Stored Payment Method is created by first creating a URL for a specific Client.
The URL is then given to the Client for the payment method (credit card, ACH, etc.) info to be input.
After the Client saves their payment info at the URL, it can be found by querying the Client again with
the storedPaymentMethods included in the request.

fields.name: id
fields.description: A unique uuidv4 value for this object.

fields.name: balance
fields.description: The balance associated with this Stored Payment Method object. All values in Cents.

fields.name: cardBrand
fields.description:
The brand of the stored payment method card if the paymentMethod is credit or debit.
Example: 'mastercard' or 'visa'.

fields.name: client
fields.description: The Client associated with this Stored Payment Method. If null, Firm will be defined.

fields.name: firm
fields.description: The Firm associated with this Stored Payment Method. If null, Client will be defined.

fields.name: lastFour
fields.description: The last four digits (this will sometimes return three) of the card number or ach bank account. Example: '1234'.

fields.name: payerName
fields.description: The name the user input on the Stored Payment Method creation page.

fields.name: nickname
fields.description:
A nickname for this specific Stored Payment Method. This should be set after the user has created the
Stored Payment Method and is used to help organize Stored Payment Methods if a single client has
multiple.

fields.name: paymentMethod
fields.description: The payment method of this Stored Payment Method. Example: 'ACH' or 'CREDIT'.

types.name: Balance
types.description:
(FROZEN) Defines payment metrics for an entity. Used to look at the historical and current
state for a specific entity (Client, Matter, etc.). A balance will always be
attached to another entity and describes the data of that parent entity.

fields.name: operatingOutstanding
fields.description: The total outstanding amount in cents for all operating Bank Accounts for the parent entity.

fields.name: operatingPaid
fields.description: The total amount paid in cents to all operating Bank Accounts for the parent entity.

fields.name: totalOutstanding
fields.description: The total outstanding amount in cents for all Bank Accounts for the parent entity.

fields.name: totalPaid
fields.description: The total amount paid in cents for all Bank Accounts for the parent entity.

fields.name: trustOutstanding
fields.description: The total outstanding amount in cents for all trust Bank Accounts for the parent entity.

fields.name: trustPaid
fields.description: The total amount paid in cents for all Bank Accounts for the parent entity.

types.name: Int
types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

types.name: DateTimeISO
types.description: A date-time string at UTC, such as 2007-12-03T10:15:30Z, compliant with the `date-time` format outlined in section 5.6 of the RFC 3339 profile of the ISO 8601 standard for representation of dates and times using the Gregorian calendar.This scalar is serialized to a string in ISO 8601 format and parsed from a string in ISO 8601 format.

types.name: Amount
types.description: (FROZEN) Defines the amount object used commonly when referring to charging a specific Bank Account.

fields.name: id
fields.description: A unique uuidv4 value for this object.

fields.name: amount
fields.description: Integer value in cents.

fields.name: bankAccount
fields.description: A uuidv4 id for the Bank Account to associate the amount with.

types.name: PaymentLink
types.description:
Defines a PaymentLink for a specific Client. This is the most common payment
vehicle used.

fields.name: id
fields.description: A unique uuidv4 value for this object.

args.name: after
args.description: The cursor to start the query from. The response will not include the object associated with the cursor.

args.name: first
args.description: The number of objects to return after the cursor.

args.name: orderDirection
args.description: The order direction the objects should be returned in. Either ASC for ascending or DESC for descending.

fields.name: amounts
fields.description:
A list of the amounts currently associated with this PaymentLink. These
represent the amount that will be charged and the bank account the money will
go into.

fields.name: balance
fields.description: The balance associated with this PaymentLink object. All values in Cents.

fields.name: client
fields.description: The Client charges to this PaymentLink will be recorded under.

fields.name: createdOn
fields.description: A Date object representing when this PaymentLink was created.

fields.name: externalId
fields.description: (Nullable) An externalId for integration partners to set and query with.

fields.name: firm
fields.description:
The Firm this PaymentLink is associated with. This Firm must also have the
bank accounts under the amounts associated with it.

fields.name: matter
fields.description: (Optional) A matter to also associate payments to this PaymentLink with.

fields.name: memo
fields.description: (Optional) A memo associated with the PaymentLink.

fields.name: partialPaymentAllowed
fields.description:
(Optional) Override for whether this PaymentLink allows partial payments. If
'null', the Firm-level paylink partial payment setting is used.

fields.name: partialPayment
fields.description:
The effective partial payment setting for this PaymentLink and whether the
Firm-level paylink partial payment setting was used.

fields.name: paymentMethodsAllowed
fields.description:
A list of the amounts currently associated with this PaymentLink. These
represent the amount that will be charged and the bank account the money will
go into.

fields.name: payments
fields.description: A connection of all Payments made on this PaymentLink.

args.name: after
args.description: The cursor to start the query from. The response will not include the object associated with the cursor.

args.name: first
args.description: The number of objects to return after the cursor.

args.name: orderDirection
args.description: The order direction the objects should be returned in. Either ASC for ascending or DESC for descending.

fields.name: sendReceipts
fields.description:
If 'false', Confido Legal will not send any receipts out for this Payment Link.
This defaults to 'true' so by default, Confido Legal will send out all receipts
for the Payment Link.

fields.name: status
fields.description:
The current status of this PaymentLink. Options: 'unpaid' | 'partially_paid' |
'paid' | 'sealed'

fields.name: surchargeEnabled
fields.description:
Indicates whether or not surcharge will be applied to credit cards for payments
on this link.

fields.name: transactions
fields.description: A connection of all Transactions made on this PaymentLink.

args.name: createdAfter
args.description: The createdOn date to grab Transaction objects after.

args.name: after
args.description: The cursor to start the query from. The response will not include the object associated with the cursor.

args.name: first
args.description: The number of objects to return after the cursor.

args.name: orderDirection
args.description: The order direction the objects should be returned in. Either ASC for ascending or DESC for descending.

fields.name: url
fields.description: The external url to the payment page for this PaymentLink.

types.name: Matter
types.description:
Defines a Matter for a specific Client. Useful for grouping Payments together
under a specific name.

fields.name: id
fields.description: A unique uuidv4 value for this object.

fields.name: balance
fields.description: Balance object holding all the historic payment information for this matter in cents.

fields.name: client
fields.description: The primary Client associated with this Matter.

fields.name: externalId
fields.description: (Nullable) An externalId for integration partners to set and query with.

fields.name: latestActivity
fields.description: (Nullable) A Date object representing the latest activity on this Matter.

fields.name: name
fields.description: A name given to this Matter.

fields.name: paymentLinks
fields.description: A Connection of all the Payment Links associated with this Matter.

args.name: afterDate
args.description: The createdOn date to grab Payment Link objects after.

args.name: after
args.description: The cursor to start the query from. The response will not include the object associated with the cursor.

args.name: first
args.description: The number of objects to return after the cursor.

args.name: orderDirection
args.description: The order direction the objects should be returned in. Either ASC for ascending or DESC for descending.

fields.name: payments
fields.description: A Connection of all the Payments made on this Matter.

args.name: afterDate
args.description: The createdOn date to grab Payment objects after.

args.name: after
args.description: The cursor to start the query from. The response will not include the object associated with the cursor.

args.name: first
args.description: The number of objects to return after the cursor.

args.name: orderDirection
args.description: The order direction the objects should be returned in. Either ASC for ascending or DESC for descending.

fields.name: secondaryClient
fields.description: (Nullable) A secondary Client associated with this Matter.

fields.name: source
fields.description: (Nullable) The original source of the Matter. If null, it was created through the Confido Legal UI.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The current status of this Matter.

args.name: after
args.description: The cursor to start the query from. The response will not include the object associated with the cursor.

args.name: first
args.description: The number of objects to return after the cursor.

args.name: orderDirection
args.description: The order direction the objects should be returned in. Either ASC for ascending or DESC for descending.

fields.name: transactions
fields.description:
A Connection of all the Transactions associated with this Matter. You can also
specify a timestamp on afterDate to gather all Transactions after that
timestamp.

args.name: afterDate
args.description: The createdOn date to grab Transaction objects after.

args.name: after
args.description: The cursor to start the query from. The response will not include the object associated with the cursor.

args.name: first
args.description: The number of objects to return after the cursor.

args.name: orderDirection
args.description: The order direction the objects should be returned in. Either ASC for ascending or DESC for descending.

fields.name: amounts
fields.description:
A list of one amount object indicating the amount that will be charged and
the bank account to which the amount will be deposited on each billing cycle.

fields.name: balance
fields.description: A Balance object with the current state of the subscription.

fields.name: client
fields.description: The Client associated with the subscription.

fields.name: id
fields.description: A unique uuidv4 value for this object.

fields.name: matter
fields.description: (Nullable) The Matter associated with the subscription.

fields.name: paymentMethods
fields.description: The payment methods to allow when activating or updating the subscription.

fields.name: schedule
fields.description:
An object defining the schedule of the subscription. When the subscription charges the
client and when the subscription is considered fulfilled.

fields.name: status
fields.description:
The current status of the subscription.

Possible values: 'active', 'canceled', 'fulfilled', 'payment_failed',
'needs_attention', and 'pending'

fields.name: storedPaymentMethod
fields.description:
(Nullable) The stored payment method associated with the subscription.

If this is null, the url should be used to activate the subscription by collecting
the client of the firm's payment method information.

fields.name: surchargeEnabled
fields.description:
A boolean value indicating whether surcharging will be added to the subscription
payments.

fields.name: upcomingPayments
fields.description:
A list of upcoming payment dates. This will be a list of string values of the
form 'YYYY-MM-DD'.

fields.name: url
fields.description:
The url to use to activate or change the stored payment method of the subscription
by collecting the firm's client's payment method information.

types.name: Firm
types.description:
The Firm object is the root entity for a law Firm. The Firm Settings indicate
important settings such as whether or not the Firm can enabled surcharging, ach, etc.

types.name: FirmStatus
types.description: The status of a firm

types.name: Contact
types.description:
(FROZEN) A Contact represents a single point of contact for a Client. A Client
can have multiple Contacts but only one primary Contact.

fields.name: id
fields.description: A unique uuidv4 value for this object.

fields.name: address
fields.description: (Nullable) The address associated with this Contact.

fields.name: email
fields.description: (Nullable) The email associated with this Contact.

fields.name: firstName
fields.description: The first name of this Contact.

fields.name: fullName
fields.description: The full name (firstName + ' ' + lastName) of this Contact.

fields.name: lastName
fields.description: (Nullable) The last name of this Contact.

fields.name: phone
fields.description: (Nullable) The phone number of this Contact.

fields.name: id
fields.description: A unique uuidv4 value for this object.

fields.name: address
fields.description: (Nullable) The address of this Client.

args.name: after

args.name: first

args.name: orderDirection

fields.name: balance
fields.description: The balance associated with this Client object. All values in Cents.

fields.name: clientName
fields.description: The name of the Client. This is the main display name for the Client.

fields.name: contacts
fields.description:
A connection containing all the Contacts associated with this Client.
The queries for Clients enable paging through all Clients.

fields.name: firstName

fields.name: lastName

fields.name: latestActivity
fields.description: A Date object representing the last time this Client was active.

fields.name: matters
fields.description: A connection of the matters associated with this client.

types.name: StandingLink
types.description: Standing links are public links that can take payment at any time.

fields.name: id
fields.description: A unique uuidv4 value for this object.

fields.name: payerNotesFieldName
fields.description: The customizable label above the notes field on the payment page

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name for the standing link

fields.name: url
fields.description: The public link used for collecting payment

types.name: Transaction
types.description:
Defines a single Transaction. This is the payment that was actually sent to
the bank, credit card processor, etc.

fields.name: id
fields.description: A unique uuidv4 value for this object.

fields.name: achStatus
fields.description:
(Nullable) If the Transaction was an ACH payment, this status represents the
current state of the Transaction, 'A' => Pending | 'B' => Originated | 'R' =>
Returned | 'S' => Settled | 'V' => Voided.

fields.name: amountProcessed
fields.description: The amount in cents that was processed for the Transaction.

fields.name: amountRefunded
fields.description: The amount in cents that was refunded for the Transaction.

fields.name: bankAccount
fields.description: The Bank Account that the Transaction was deposited into.

fields.name: events
fields.description: A list of events related to the transaction. Only available when querying an individual transaction.

fields.name: originalTransactionId
fields.description: The id of the original transaction that was refunded, voided, or returned.

fields.name: payRequest
fields.description: External ID for tracking this payment in partner systems

fields.name: processingEnv
fields.description: The environment this transaction was processed in. Possible values: 'mock', 'sandbox', 'production'.

fields.name: settledOn
fields.description:
(Nullable) If the Transaction was an ACH payment, this will be set to a
timestamp for when the payment was settled.

fields.name: status
fields.description:
The current status of the Transaction. Possible values: 'error' |
'originated' | 'pending' | 'refunded' | 'returned' | 'reversed' |
'settled' | 'successful' | 'voided'.

fields.name: status_v2
fields.description: The current status of the Transaction.

fields.name: surchargeAmount
fields.description:
The amount in cents that was paid for surcharging. This is not included in the
amount so the total amount processed would be amount + surchargeAmount.

fields.name: surchargeAmountRefunded
fields.description:
The amount in cents that was refunded for surcharging. This is not included in
the amountRefunded so the total amount refunded would be amountRefunded +
surchargeAmountRefunded.

fields.name: type
fields.description:
The type of the transaction. Possible values: 'achPayment' | 'ccPayment' |
'chargeback' | 'chargeback_reversal' | 'error' | 'manualPayment' | 'refund' |
'reverse' | 'transfer' | 'void'.

fields.name: updatedOn
fields.description: A Date object representing the last time this Transaction was updated.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique identifier for the disbursement.

fields.name: allowedMethods
fields.description: The allowed disbursement methods for accepting this disbursement. If null, the firm's default methods will be used.

fields.name: amount
fields.description: Amount in cents to be disbursed. If the client selects an instant disbursement method, the client will receive this amount minus the instant fee.

fields.name: authorizedIdentities
fields.description: The identities that, when authorized, will be able to access the disbursement.

fields.name: client
fields.description: The client to associate with the disbursement. Some enhanced security checks will use information from the client to validate the payee.

fields.name: depositEta
fields.description: The estimated deposit date for the disbursement. Only available for certain disbursement statuses and methods.

fields.name: expectedDeliveryDate
fields.description: The expected delivery date shown to the client. This is the date from payoutDetails that the client sees.

fields.name: enhancedSecurity
fields.description: Refer to field usingDefaultEnhancedSecurity to determine if this value overrides the firm's default.

fields.name: expiryDays
fields.description: The number of days after the disbursement is approved that it will expire. If null, the firm's default value will be used.

fields.name: expiresAt
fields.description: The date the disbursement will expire. This will be null until the disbursement is approved.

fields.name: failedTxnCount
fields.description: Number of failed transaction attempts for this disbursement.

fields.name: fundingAccount
fields.description: The bank account from which the disbursement will be funded.

fields.name: maxFailedTxns
fields.description: Maximum number of failed transaction attempts before the disbursement is marked FAILED. Admins can increment to allow more attempts.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Extra data to attach to the disbursement. At acceptance, this data is copied to the transaction. Only flat objects are allowed (e.g., { foo: 'bar' }); nested objects will be rejected.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The current status of the disbursement.

fields.name: url
fields.description: The URL for accessing the disbursement. Only available after the disbursement has been approved.

fields.name: usingDefaultEnhancedSecurity
fields.description: Indicates if the enhancedSecurity value uses the firm's default. If false, the disbursement has its own value and will be unaffected by changes to the firm's default value.

fields.name: usingDefaultExpiryDays
fields.description: Indicates if the expiryDays value uses the firm's default. If false, the disbursement has its own value and will be unaffected by changes to the firm's default value.

fields.name: usingDefaultMethods
fields.description: Indicates if the allowedMethods value use the firm's default. If false, the disbursement has its own value and will be unaffected by changes to the firm's default values.

fields.name: source
fields.description: Used to determine if the identity was pulled from a known source.

types.name: JSON
types.description: The `JSON` scalar type represents JSON values as specified by [ECMA-404](http://www.ecma-international.org/publications/files/ECMA-ST/ECMA-404.pdf).

fields.name: id
fields.description: Event uuid

fields.name: date
fields.description: When the event occurred

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of event

fields.name: typeId
fields.description: Uuid for the primary entity

fields.name: creator
fields.description: Display name of the user or system that triggered this event

fields.name: data
fields.description: Data associated with transaction events

fields.name: newProps
fields.description: Data just after event occurred

args.name: ids
args.description: List of client IDs to filter by.

args.name: clientName
args.description: Filter clients by a partial match on name (case insensitive).

args.name: createdOn
args.description: Filter clients by creation date.

args.name: email
args.description: Filter clients by a partial match on email address (case insensitive).

args.name: externalIds
args.description: List of external IDs to filter by.

args.name: firstName
args.description: Filter clients by a partial match on first name (case insensitive).

args.name: lastName
args.description: Filter clients by a partial match on last name (case insensitive).

args.name: latestActivity
args.description: Filter clients by latest activity date.

args.name: limit
args.description: Number of clients to return. Maximum we will return in a query is 10,000.

args.name: offset
args.description: Number of clients to skip before starting to collect the result set.

args.name: orderBy
args.description: Fields to order the results by. Available values are 'clientName', 'createdOn', and 'latestActivity'. Defaults to 'clientName'.

args.name: orderDir
args.description: Direction to order the results by (ASC or DESC). Defaults to 'ASC'.

args.name: phone
args.description: Filter clients by a partial match on phone number (case insensitive).

args.name: authorizedIdentities
args.description: Each string is used in a like comparison with wild cards on each side for the authorized identity values.

args.name: clientIds
args.description: A list of client ids that must be on the disbursements

args.name: disbursementNumber
args.description: Filter by disbursement number. Supports partial matching and multiple numbers separated by spaces (e.g., "1001", "100", "1001 1003 1120").

args.name: matterIds
args.description: A list of matter ids that must be on the disbursements

args.name: orderBy
args.description: Orders the disbursements in order of the fields. Possible fields: 'amount' | 'createdOn'

args.name: orderDir
args.description: Defaults to 'DESC'.

args.name: statuses
args.description: List statuses to filter by.

fields.name: firmsList
fields.description: Request a list of firms. Only valid for partner tokens. The maximum limit is 1,000.

args.name: limit

args.name: name

args.name: skipTotal
args.description: Whether to skip the total count of matching firms. Defaults to false. Set to true to skip the total count query for faster lookups.

args.name: clientId
args.description:
The id for the Client that you would like to associate the new card or bank account
information with. This is the Client you will find the Stored Payment Method under.

args.name: orderDir
args.description: Direction to order the statements by.

args.name: endMonth
args.description: Month to end the list of statements with in the format YYYY-MM.

args.name: startMonth
args.description: Month to begin the list of statements with in the format YYYY-MM.

args.name: offset
args.description: The number of statements to skip.

args.name: aggregatePaymentLinkIds
args.description: Transactions will all be associated with one of these aggregate payment link ids.

args.name: amountProcessed
args.description: Filter transactions by the amount processed.

args.name: amountRefunded
args.description: Filter transactions by the amount refunded.

args.name: bankAccountIds
args.description: Transactions will all be associated with one of these bank account ids.

args.name: cardBrands
args.description: Filter transactions by card brand.

args.name: clientIds
args.description: Transactions will all be associated with one of these client ids.

args.name: createdOn
args.description: Filter transactions by creation date.

args.name: depositIds
args.description: Transactions will all be associated with one of these deposit ids.

args.name: excludeStatuses
args.description: List of transaction status to exclude. Has precedence over the values defined in "status".

args.name: excludeTypes
args.description: List of transaction types to exclude. Has precedence over the values defined in "types".

args.name: firmIds
args.description: Filters transactions to those belonging to the specified firms. Only valid when using a partner token. When using a firm token, transactions are automatically limited to that firm, and this parameter is ignored.

args.name: partnerIds
args.description: Filters transactions to those belonging to the specified partners. Only valid when using a system admin token. When using a partner token, transactions are automatically limited to that partner.

args.name: lastFour
args.description: Filter transactions by the last four digits of the card number.

args.name: limit
args.description: Number of transactions to return. Maximum we will return in a query is 10,000.

args.name: matterIds
args.description: Transactions will all be associated with one of these matter ids.

args.name: offset
args.description: Number of clients to skip before starting to collect the result set.

args.name: orderBy
args.description: Orders the transactions in order of the fields. Possible fields: amountProcessed | createdOn

args.name: orderDir
args.description: Defaults to 'DESC'.

args.name: payerEmail
args.description: Transactions will have a payer email that contains this string (case insensitive).

args.name: payerName
args.description: Transactions will have a payer name that contains this string (case insensitive).

args.name: paymentMethods
args.description: List payment methods to filter by.

args.name: paymentIds
args.description: Transactions will all be associated with one of these payment ids.

args.name: paymentLinkIds
args.description: Transactions will all be associated with one of these payment link ids.

args.name: statuses
args.description: List statuses to filter by.

args.name: storedPaymentMethodIds
args.description: Transactions will all be associated with one of these stored payment method ids.

args.name: surchargeAmount
args.description: Filter transactions by surcharge amount.

args.name: types
args.description: List types to filter by.

args.description: A unique uuidv4 value for this object.

inputFields.name: gt
inputFields.description: Any string value that the node Date object can parse. Because of timestamp precision, this is inclusive.

inputFields.name: lt
inputFields.description: Any string value that the node Date object can parse. Because of timestamp precision, this is exclusive.

fields.name: errorDescription
fields.description: Will be populated for ACCEPTED type events that were failures.

fields.name: defaultDescription
fields.description: Default description of the error for the user.

fields.name: defaultLink
fields.description: Default link to help/resolution guide.

fields.name: firmDescription
fields.description: Firm-specific override for the error description.

fields.name: firmLink
fields.description: Firm-specific override for the help link.

fields.name: acceptEvents
fields.description: Acceptance events for this document, most recent first.

fields.name: originalTransactions
fields.description: All original transactions that were refunded by the mutation. Will be null if status is still pending.

fields.name: refundRequest
fields.description: A reference back to this specific refund request.

fields.name: refundTransactions
fields.description: The new refund type transactions created by the mutation. Will be null if status is still pending.

types.name: Statement
types.description: The firm's statement for a specific month.

fields.name: additionalCredits
fields.description: Additional credits made to the firm this month.

fields.name: additionalFees
fields.description: Additional fees charged to the firm this month.

fields.name: month
fields.description: The month for this statement in the format YYYY-MM

types.name: BigInt
types.description: The `BigInt` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values.

fields.name: canVoid
fields.description: Whether or not the transaction can be voided

fields.name: transactions
fields.description: All transactions that will be voided as a result of the request

fields.name: voidRequests
fields.description: All void requests associated with the transaction

fields.name: originalTransactions
fields.description: The original transactions that were voided. Null if request is still pending or failed.

fields.name: voidRequest
fields.description: A reference back to this specific void request.

fields.name: voidTransactions
fields.description: The new void transactions created. Null if request is still pending or failed.

fields.name: addAddress
fields.deprecationReason: All addresses are associated with an entity and can be added through the parent entity.

fields.name: removeAddress
fields.deprecationReason: All addresses are associated with an entity and can be removed through the parent entity.

fields.name: updateAddress
fields.deprecationReason: All addresses are associated with an entity and can be updated through the parent entity.

args.name: input
args.description: The input object must define the clientName and the firmId to be a valid execution.

args.name: input
args.description: The id must define a Client that can be found in our database.

fields.name: documentAccept
fields.description: Record acceptance of a document; creates an audit accept event.

fields.name: sandboxOnlyActivateFirm
fields.description: This mutation is for use in development only. It simulates the Firm being successfully onboarded.

fields.name: sandboxOnlyFillOnboardingData
fields.description: This mutation is for use in development only. It fills the Firm's onboarding form with mock data.

fields.name: sandboxOnlySendFirmToReview
fields.description: This mutation is for use in development only. It simulates the firm application being under review.

fields.name: sandboxOnlySubmitOnboardingData
fields.description: This mutation is for use in development only. It fills the Firm's onboarding form with mock data and submits it.

args.name: input
args.description: Either bankAccountId or paymentLinkId may be defined.

args.name: input
args.description:
The object that defines how to execute the payment. The storedPaymentMethodId will found
on the Client object after a user has input their card or bank account information at
the Stored Payment Method url. Either amount, operating, or trust must be defined in Cents.

fields.name: storedPaymentMethodRefund
fields.description: To enable, contact Confido Legal support. Issuing refunds without a linked transaction has additional underwriting requirements.

fields.name: sandboxOnlyCreateMockStatement
fields.description: This mutation is for use in development only. It creates a statement with random values for the firm on the specified month.

args.name: input
args.description: The month in the format YYYY-MM to create a statement for.

fields.name: sandboxOnlyUpdateMockStatement
fields.description: This mutation is for use in development only. It updates a statement with new random values to trigger an update statement webhook.

types.name: AddAddressInput
types.description:
Defines the input parameters for adding a new Address. This is
used internally through adding other entities such as Clients and
Contacts.

inputFields.name: line1

inputFields.name: line2

inputFields.name: state
inputFields.description: (Optional) The state for this Address.

inputFields.name: zip
inputFields.description: (Optional) The zip for this Address.

inputFields.name: amount
inputFields.description: The amount in cents to disburse to the client. Must be greater than $0.00 and less than $100,000

inputFields.name: enhancedSecurity
inputFields.description: If null, uses firm's default security settings.

inputFields.name: expiryDays
inputFields.description: If null, uses firm's default expiry days settings

inputFields.name: fundingAccountId
inputFields.description: The id of the bank account to pull the funds out of for the disbursement.

inputFields.description: Optional client-supplied ID for the new disbursement. Must be a valid UUIDv4 string. If null, the ID will be generated.

inputFields.name: status
inputFields.description: Defaults to 'DRAFT'.

inputFields.name: source
inputFields.description: Used to determine if the identity was pulled from a known source. Defaults to MANUAL.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: A uuid v4. This id is first used to lookup the client, then used as the id of a new client if one is not found.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: A uuid v4. This id is first used to lookup the matter, then used as the id of a new matter if one is not found.

inputFields.name: amount
inputFields.description: The amount in cents to disburse to the client. Must be greater than $0.00 and less than $100,000

inputFields.name: enhancedSecurity
inputFields.description: If null, uses firm's default enhanced security settings.

inputFields.name: expiryDays
inputFields.description: If null, uses firm's default expiry days settings

inputFields.name: maxFailedTxns
inputFields.description: Max failed transaction attempts before disbursement is set to FAILED. FIRM_ADMIN only. Increase to allow more attempts.

inputFields.name: fundingAccountId
inputFields.description: The id of the bank account to pull the funds out of for the disbursement.

inputFields.name: status
inputFields.description: Defaults to 'DRAFT'.

types.name: AddMatterInput

Defines the input object for adding a Matter. The Client and secondary Client
can not be changed in the future so be sure to assign them correctly.

inputFields.description: A unique uuidv4 value for this object.

inputFields.name: externalId
inputFields.description: (Optional) An externalId for querying down the road.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: A descriptive name for the Matter.

inputFields.name: secondaryClientId
inputFields.description: (Optional) A secondary Client to associate with the Matter.

inputFields.description: (Required) The uuidv4 id of the Matter.

inputFields.name: name

types.name: AddAmountInput
types.description: Defines the structure for adding an amount.

inputFields.name: amount
inputFields.description: (Required) Integer value in cents.

inputFields.name: bankAccountId
inputFields.description: (Required) A uuidv4 id for the Bank Account to associate the amount with.

types.name: RecordManualPaymentInput
types.description: The object for recording a manual payment on a PaymentLink.

inputFields.name: amounts
inputFields.description:
A list of amounts to make the manual payment on. If operating or trust
is also defined, a UserInputError will be thrown.

inputFields.name: billingEmail
inputFields.description: [Optional] The email address to link to the manual payment.

inputFields.name: billingName
inputFields.description: [Optional] The name to link to the manual payment.

inputFields.name: operating
inputFields.description:
The amount in cents to deposit into the default operating account. If
amounts is also defined, a UserInputError will be thrown.

inputFields.name: paymentLinkId
inputFields.description: [Required] The id of the PaymentLink to make the manual payment on.

inputFields.name: statement
inputFields.description: [Optional] A statement to add to the manual payment for future reference.

inputFields.name: trust
inputFields.description:
The amount in cents to deposit into the default trust account. If
amounts is also defined, a UserInputError will be thrown.

inputFields.description: Internal only; not used for integrations.

inputFields.description: Optional external identifier for the payment request

inputFields.name: sendReceipt
inputFields.description: sendReceipt=true will definitely send a receipt and sendReceipt=false will definitely not send a receipt. Unset will fallback to Firm's defaults.

inputFields.name: surchargeEnabled
inputFields.description: Surcharge must be allowed at the firm level. If not null, overrides the default surcharge settings for this payment.

inputFields.name: surchargeRegion
inputFields.description: Overrides the default surcharge region for this payment. The surcharge region determines the surcharge rate.

inputFields.description: sendReceipt=true will definitely send a receipt and sendReceipt=false will definitely not send a receipt. Unset will fallback to Firm's defaults.

types.name: SPMPaymentInput
types.description:
The SPM Payment Input is used to create a new payment on the Stored Payment Method
with the corresponding id. A valid input must define either operating, trust, or
the amount. If operating or trust are defined, the Firm's default Bank Account for
that category will be used.

inputFields.name: amount
inputFields.description:
An amount object that defines the amount of the payment and which Bank Account to
deposit the payment into.

inputFields.name: emailForReceipt
inputFields.description:
(Optional) Defines an email to send a receipt to for the payment on
the Stored Payment Method. Email must be valid or the request will error out.

inputFields.name: externalId
inputFields.description: Optional external identifier for the payment request

inputFields.name: matterId
inputFields.description: (Optional) An optional Matter id to link the payment to.

inputFields.name: operating
inputFields.description:
An integer value in cents to charge the Stored Payment Method and deposit into the default
operating Bank Account.

inputFields.name: sendReceipt
inputFields.description: sendReceipt=true will definitely send a receipt and sendReceipt=false will definitely not send a receipt. Unset will fallback to Firm's defaults.

inputFields.name: storedPaymentMethodId
inputFields.description: The id of the Stored Payment Method to charge.

inputFields.name: surchargeEnabled
inputFields.description:
(Optional) Whether or not to add surcharging to the charge. If null, the Firm's default setting
will be used.

inputFields.name: surchargeRegion
inputFields.description: Override the surcharge region for this payment. The surcharge region determines the applicable surcharge rate.

inputFields.name: trust
inputFields.description:
An integer value in cents to charge the Stored Payment Method and deposit into the default
trust Bank Account.

inputFields.name: amount
inputFields.description: The amount in cents to refund

inputFields.name: bankAccountId
inputFields.description: The id of the bank account to refund the money from

inputFields.name: spmId
inputFields.description: The id of the stored payment method to send the refund to

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: (Required) The uuidv4 id of the Stored Payment Method.

inputFields.name: nickname
inputFields.description:
(Required) A string value representing a new name for the Stored
Payment Method.

inputFields.name: month
inputFields.description: The month in the format YYYY-MM to create a statement for.

inputFields.name: amounts
inputFields.description:
(Optional) A list of one amount object. This represents the amount that will be charged
on each billing cycle. Subscriptions can only have a single bank account/amount
at this time.

If this field is provided, operating and trust must be omitted.

inputFields.name: clientId
inputFields.description: The id of a Client to associate the subscription with.

inputFields.name: matterId
inputFields.description: (Optional) The id of a Matter to associate the subscription with.

inputFields.name: paymentMethods
inputFields.description:
(Optional) The payment methods to allow a client to input when activating the subscription.

If this is omitted, the default firm payment methods will be used.

inputFields.name: operating
inputFields.description:
(Optional) The amount in cents to charge the client on each billing cycle. The amount
will be deposited into the firm's default operating account.

If this field is provided, amounts and trust must be omitted.

inputFields.name: schedule
inputFields.description:
An object defining the schedule of the subscription. When the subscription charges the
client and when the subscription is considered fulfilled.

inputFields.name: startDate
inputFields.description:
The date in which the first subscription charge will be made. This will be
a string value of the form 'YYYY-MM-DD'.

inputFields.name: storedPaymentMethodId
inputFields.description:
(Optional) The id of a stored payment method to set on the subscription. If this is
provided, the status of the subscription will be 'active' immediately.

If this is omitted, the subscription url that is returned should be used
to collect the payment method from a firm's client.

inputFields.name: trust
inputFields.description:
(Optional) The amount in cents to charge the client on each billing cycle. The amount
will be deposited into the firm's default trust account.

If this field is provided, amounts and operating must be omitted.

inputFields.name: ends
inputFields.description:
An object that defines when the subscription ends. This object should contain one (and
only one) of the following keys: 'amountPaid', 'never', 'on', or 'payments'.

When the subscription ends is updated, the original termination type can not be changed.
For example, if the subscription ends was 'amountPaid', you can not update the ends to
'never' or 'on'.

inputFields.name: frequency
inputFields.description:
The frequency of the subscription. This object should contain one (and only one) of the
following keys: 'biweekly', 'monthly', 'weekly', or 'yearly'.

inputFields.name: amountPaid
inputFields.description:
(Optional) The amount in cents that needs to be paid on the subscription for the
subscription to be fulfilled.

inputFields.name: never
inputFields.description: (Optional) If true, the subscription will continue to charge the client indefinitely.

inputFields.name: on
inputFields.description:
(Optional) The end date for the subscription. The client will be charged until this
date is reached. This should be a date of the form 'YYYY-MM-DD'.

inputFields.name: payments
inputFields.description:
(Optional) The number of payments the client needs to be charged for the subscription
to be fulfilled.

inputFields.name: biweekly
inputFields.description:
(Optional) A list of one day of the week on which the client will be charged every
other week. Note, future work will allow for multiple days of the week to be specified.

Possible values: 'monday', 'tuesday', 'wednesday', 'thursday', 'friday', 'saturday',
and 'sunday'.

inputFields.name: monthly
inputFields.description:
(Optional) A list of one or two days of the month on which the client will be charged.
Note, future work will allow for 3+ days of the month to be specified.

Possible values: integers between 1 and 31.

inputFields.name: weekly
inputFields.description:
(Optional) A list of one day of the week on which the client will be charged every
week. Note, future work will allow for multiple days of the week to be specified.

Possible values: 'monday', 'tuesday', 'wednesday', 'thursday', 'friday', 'saturday',
and 'sunday'.

inputFields.name: yearly
inputFields.description:
(Optional) A list of one day of the year on which the client will be charged. Note,
future work will allow for multiple days of the year to be specified.

Possible values: dates of the form 'MM-DD' (e.g. '01-01' for January 1st).

inputFields.name: amounts
inputFields.description:
(Optional) A list of one amount object. This represents the amount that will be charged
on each billing cycle. Subscriptions can only have a single bank account/amount
at this time.

If this field is provided, operating and trust must be omitted.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: (Required) The uuidv4 id of the Subscription.

inputFields.name: matterId
inputFields.description: (Optional) The id of a Matter to associate the subscription with.

inputFields.name: operating
inputFields.description:
(Optional) The amount in cents to charge the client on each billing cycle. The amount
will be deposited into the firm's default operating account.

If this field is provided, amounts and trust must be omitted.

inputFields.name: schedule
inputFields.description:
(Optional) 
An object defining the schedule of the subscription. When the subscription charges the
client and when the subscription is considered fulfilled.

inputFields.name: storedPaymentMethodId
inputFields.description:
(Optional) The id of a stored payment method to set on the subscription. If this is
provided, the status of the subscription will be 'active' immediately.

If this is omitted, the subscription url that is returned should be used
to collect the payment method from a firm's client.

inputFields.name: trust
inputFields.description:
(Optional) The amount in cents to charge the client on each billing cycle. The amount
will be deposited into the firm's default trust account.

If this field is provided, amounts and operating must be omitted.

inputFields.name: ends
inputFields.description:
(Optional) 
An object that defines when the subscription ends. This object should contain one (and
only one) of the following keys: 'amountPaid', 'never', 'on', or 'payments'.

When the subscription ends is updated, the original termination type can not be changed.
For example, if the subscription ends was 'amountPaid', you can not update the ends to
'never' or 'on'.

inputFields.name: frequency
inputFields.description:
(Optional) 
The frequency of the subscription. This object should contain one (and only one) of the
following keys: 'biweekly', 'monthly', 'weekly', or 'yearly'.

inputFields.name: amount
inputFields.description: The amount in cents to refund for a partial refund. If null, the entire transaction will be refunded.

inputFields.name: sendReceipt
inputFields.description: sendReceipt=true will definitely send a receipt and sendReceipt=false will definitely not send a receipt. Unset will fallback to Firm's defaults.

inputFields.name: transactionId
inputFields.description: The id of the transaction to refund.

inputFields.name: transactionId
inputFields.description: The id of the transaction to trigger an ACH return for.

inputFields.name: transactionId
inputFields.description: The id of the card payment transaction to simulate an Adyen chargeback for.

inputFields.name: amount
inputFields.description: Optional chargeback amount in cents. Defaults to full transaction amount.

inputFields.name: transactionId
inputFields.description: The id of the chargeback transaction to simulate an Adyen chargeback reversal for.

inputFields.name: amount
inputFields.description: Optional chargeback reversal amount in cents. Defaults to full transaction amount.

inputFields.name: transactionId
inputFields.description: The id of the chargeback_reversal transaction to simulate a pre-arbitration lost (second chargeback) for.

inputFields.name: amount
inputFields.description: Optional amount in cents for the second chargeback. Defaults to full transaction amount.

inputFields.name: sendReceipt
inputFields.description: sendReceipt=true will definitely send a receipt and sendReceipt=false will definitely not send a receipt. Unset will fallback to Firm's defaults.

inputFields.name: transactionId
inputFields.description: The id of the transaction to void.

inputFields.name: sendReceipt
inputFields.description: sendReceipt=true will definitely send a receipt and sendReceipt=false will definitely not send a receipt. Unset will fallback to Firm's defaults.

inputFields.name: transactionId
inputFields.description: The id of the transaction to void or refund.

fields.name: originalTransactions
fields.description: All original transactions that were refunded by the mutation. Will be null if status is still pending.

fields.name: refundRequest
fields.description: A reference back to this specific refund request.

fields.name: refundTransactions
fields.description: The new refund type transactions created by the mutation. Will be null if status is still pending.

inputFields.name: amount
inputFields.description: The amount in cents to refund for a partial refund. If null, the max will be refunded.

inputFields.name: externalId
inputFields.description: Optional external identifier for the refund request. Must be unique.

inputFields.name: sendReceipt
inputFields.description: If not null, overrides the firm's default receipt behavior.

inputFields.name: transactionId
inputFields.description: The id of the transaction to refund.

fields.name: originalTransactions
fields.description: The original transactions that were voided. Null if request is still pending or failed.

fields.name: voidRequest
fields.description: A reference back to this specific void request.

fields.name: voidTransactions
fields.description: The new void transactions created. Null if request is still pending or failed.

inputFields.name: externalId
inputFields.description: Optional external identifier for the void request. Must be unique.

inputFields.name: sendReceipt
inputFields.description: If not null, overrides the firm's default receipt behavior.

inputFields.name: transactionId
inputFields.description: The id of the transaction to void.

fields.name: originalTransactions
fields.description: The original transactions that were voided or refunded. Null if request is still pending or failed.

fields.name: voidOrRefundTransactions
fields.description: The new void or refund transactions created. Null if request is still pending or failed.

inputFields.name: externalId
inputFields.description: Optional external identifier for the void or refund request. Must be unique.

inputFields.name: sendReceipt
inputFields.description: If not null, overrides the firm's default receipt behavior.

inputFields.name: transactionId
inputFields.description: The id of the transaction to void or refund.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

args.name: if
args.description: Included when true.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

args.name: if
args.description: Skipped when true.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

directives.name: oneOf
directives.description: Indicates exactly one field must be supplied and this field must not be `null`.
